title: Unit and integration tests deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Added digest-pinned deterministic executable evidence for Unit and integration tests; AI-assisted draft remains human-unreviewed and exam-ineligible.

items.title: Unit and integration tests: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? High line coverage proves the class contract and every edge case are correct.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Coverage measures execution, not correctness; derive assertions from contracts, equivalence classes, boundaries, failures, and invariants.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Coverage measures execution, not correctness; derive assertions from contracts, equivalence classes, boundaries, failures, and invariants.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Coverage measures execution, not correctness; derive assertions from contracts, equivalence classes, boundaries, failures, and invariants.

items.title: Unit and integration tests: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing CPP checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: High line coverage proves the class contract and every edge case are correct.

feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. Coverage measures execution, not correctness; derive assertions from contracts, equivalence classes, boundaries, failures, and invariants.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact CPP token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.

items.template: Coverage measures execution, not correctness; derive assertions from {{key-rule}}, equivalence classes, boundaries, failures, and invariants.

items.title: Unit and integration tests: executable application
items.prompt: Implement is_even, keep the supplied embedded checks for zero, negative, odd, and even values, then classify one input as even or odd only after the checks pass.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: embedded boundary assertions plus observable classification

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises embedded boundary assertions plus observable classification under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
